[The Complete Guide to Claude Code settings.json — Every Setting Explained with Practical Examples | Claude Lab](https://claudelab.net/en/articles/claude-code/claude-code-settings-json-complete-guide)\n2. [Claude Code Settings Reference (Complete Config Guide)](https://claudefa.st/blog/guide/settings-reference)\n5. [Your Claude Code Settings Will Die With Your Machine - DEV Community](https://dev.to/ithiria894/your-claude-code-settings-will-die-with-your-machine-42on)\n\nSynthesis:\n**The Claude Code user/global settings.json configuration file is &#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96;.**  \nIt applies to all projects for permissions, hooks, environment variables, and model settings. Create it manually: &#96;mkdir -p ~/.claude \u0026\u0026 touch ~/.claude/settings.json&#96;. (Note: &#96;~/.claude.json&#96; is separate for internal state like OAuth and preferences.)[1][2][3][4]\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eThe Complete Guide to Claude Code settings.json — Every Setting Explained with Practical Examples | Claude Lab\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://claudelab.net/en/articles/claude-code/claude-code-settings-json-complete-guide\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e| Scope | File Location | Who It Affects | Shared with Team? | |-------|--------------|----------------|-------------------| | Managed | Server-managed settings, MDM policies | All users in the organization | Deployed by IT | | User |&#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96;| You, across all projects | Personal only | | Project |&#96;.claude/settings.json&#96;| All collaborators on this repo | Committed to Git | | Local |&#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96;| You, in this repo only | Personal only |\n[...]\nTo create settings files manually:\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n# Create user settings\nmkdir -p ~/.claude\ntouch ~/.claude/settings.json\n \n# Create project settings\nmkdir -p .claude\ntouch .claude/settings.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\n### What's the difference between settings.json and .claude.json?\n[...]\n&#96;settings.json&#96; is the official configuration file for controlling permissions, hooks, environment variables, and model settings. On the other hand,&#96;.claude.json&#96;(located in your home directory) stores internal state like OAuth sessions, theme preferences, notification settings, and MCP server configurations. For customization purposes, always use&#96;settings.json&#96;.\n[...]\nUse project settings (&#96;.claude/settings.json&#96;) for rules that should apply to your entire team — permissions, hooks, and standardized tooling. Use local settings (&#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96;) for personal preferences or machine-specific configurations. Local settings are automatically added to&#96;.gitignore&#96;, so they won't be committed to your repository.\n[...]\nYes. On macOS, place your configuration at&#96;/Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/managed-settings.json&#96;, and on Linux at&#96;/etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json&#96;. However, for personal use, the user settings file (&#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96;) is typically sufficient. Managed settings are primarily designed for IT departments to enforce organizational policies at scale.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eClaude Code settings - Claude Code Docs\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e| Feature | User location | Project location | Local location |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| Settings | &#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96; | &#96;.claude/settings.json&#96; | &#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96; |\n| Subagents | &#96;~/.claude/agents/&#96; | &#96;.claude/agents/&#96; | None |\n| MCP servers | &#96;~/.claude.json&#96; | &#96;.mcp.json&#96; | &#96;~/.claude.json&#96; (per-project) |\n| Plugins | &#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96; | &#96;.claude/settings.json&#96; | &#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96; |\n| CLAUDE.md | &#96;~/.claude/CLAUDE.md&#96; | &#96;CLAUDE.md&#96; or &#96;.claude/CLAUDE.md&#96; | &#96;CLAUDE.local.md&#96; |\n[...]\nThe &#96;settings.json&#96; file is the official mechanism for configuring Claude\nCode through hierarchical settings:\n[...]\n- User settings are defined in &#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96; and apply to all\nprojects.\n- Project settings are saved in your project directory:\n- &#96;.claude/settings.json&#96; for settings that are checked into source control and shared with your team\n- &#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96; for settings that are not checked in, useful for personal preferences and experimentation. Claude Code will configure git to ignore &#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96; when it is created.\n[...]\n- Other configuration is stored in &#96;~/.claude.json&#96;. This file contains your OAuth session, MCP server configurations for user and local scopes, per-project state (allowed tools, trust settings), and various caches. Project-scoped MCP servers are stored separately in &#96;.mcp.json&#96;.\n[...]\n### Global config settings\n[...]\nThese settings are stored in &#96;~/.claude.json&#96; rather than &#96;settings.json&#96;. Adding them to &#96;settings.json&#96; will trigger a schema validation error.\n[...]\n1. User\n[...]\n(&#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96;)\n\n-\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eClaude Code Settings — Claude\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://claude.yourdocs.dev/docs/claude-code/settings\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eThe &#96;settings.json&#96; file is our official mechanism for configuring Claude\nCode through hierarchical settings:\n[...]\n- User settings are defined in &#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96; and apply to all\nprojects.\n- Project settings are saved in your project directory:\n- &#96;.claude/settings.json&#96; for settings that are checked into source control and shared with your team\n- &#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96; for settings that are not checked in, useful for personal preferences and experimentation. Claude Code will configure git to ignore &#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96; when it is created.\n[...]\nproject settings (&#96;.claude/settings.local\n[...]\n1. Shared project settings (&#96;.claude/settings.json&#96;)\n[...]\n- Team-\n[...]\n1. User settings (&#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96;)\n\n- Personal global settings\n[...]\nTo prevent Claude Code from accessing files containing sensitive information (e.g., API keys, secrets, environment files), use the &#96;permissions.deny&#96; setting in your &#96;.claude/settings.json&#96; file:\n[...]\n~/.cla\n[...]\n/settings.json\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eClaude Code Settings Reference (Complete Config Guide)\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://claudefa.st/blog/guide/settings-reference\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e| Priority | Scope | Location | Who It Affects | Shared? |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| 1 | Managed | System directories | All users on machine | Yes (IT-deployed) |\n| 2 | Command line | CLI flags | Current session only | No |\n| 3 | Local | &#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96; | You, this project | No (gitignored) |\n| 4 | Project | &#96;.claude/settings.json&#96; | All collaborators | Yes (in git) |\n| 5 | User | &#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96; | You, all projects | No |\n[...]\n## Complete Settings File Locations\n[...]\nto a specific file at each scope:\n[...]\n| Feature | User | Project (shared) | Local (personal) |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| Settings | &#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96; | &#96;.claude/settings.json&#96; | &#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96; |\n| Subagents | &#96;~/.claude/agents/&#96; | &#96;.claude/agents/&#96; | N/A |\n| MCP servers | &#96;~/.claude.json&#96; | &#96;.mcp.json&#96; | &#96;~/.claude.json&#96;(per-project) |\n| Plugins | &#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96; | &#96;.claude/settings.json&#96; | &#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96; |\n| CLAUDE.md | &#96;~/.claude/CLAUDE.md&#96; | &#96;CLAUDE.md&#96; or&#96;.claude/CLAUDE.md&#96; | &#96;CLAUDE.local.md&#96; |\n[...]\nA minimal&#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96; for individual developers:\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n{\n  \"$schema\": \"https://json-schema.org/claude-code-settings.json\",\n  \"permissions\": {\n    \"allow\": [\n      \"Bash(npm run *)\",\n      \"Bash(git status)\",\n      \"Bash(git diff *)\",\n      \"Bash(git log *)\"\n    ],\n    \"deny\": [\"Read(./.env)\", \"Read(./\n[...]\nenv.*)\", \"Read(~/.ssh/**)\"]\n  },\n  \"showTurnDuration\": true,\n  \"autoUpdatesChannel\": \"stable\"\n}\n[...]\nA&#96;.claude/settings.json&#96; committed to your repo:\n[...]\nYou want to test a hook before proposing it to the team. Add it to&#96;.claude/settings.local.json&#96;. It takes priority over the shared&#96;.claude/settings.json\n[...]\nbut only applies to you.\n[...]\nso there's\n[...]\nThis applies to&#96;settings.json&#96;,&#96;.claude.json\n[...]\nand other core config files. Combined\n[...]\nthe local scope for experimentation, you have a solid safety net for trying new configurations.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eYour Claude Code Settings Will Die With Your Machine - DEV Community\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://dev.to/ithiria894/your-claude-code-settings-will-die-with-your-machine-42on\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e. &#96;&#96;&#96; ~/.claude/ ├── CLAUDE.md # your global instructions ├── settings.json # global settings ├── memories/ # things Claude remembers about you ├── skills/ # custom skills/commands ├── rules/ # behavior rules ├── plans/ # saved plans ├── projects/ # per-project memories, encoded paths │ ├── -home-you-project-a/ │ │ └── memory/ │ └── -home-you-project-b/ │ └── memory/ # PLUS every project directory: ~/myproject/.claude/ ├── CLAUDE.md ├── settings.json ├── skills/ └── rules/ &#96;&#96;&#96; A typical power user has 600+ items spread across 8+ scopes. Most of these files you probably never manually touched. Claude Code creates them quietly in the background as you work. And none of it is backed up by default. Lose the machine, lose everything. The memories Claude built about your codebase. The skills you wrote. The MCP configurations that took you an afternoon to get right. All of it. Manual backup is impractical because you'd have to know every location, remember to run it, and do it consistently. That last part is what actually kills you. ## What claude-code-backup does It's a tool that discovers every Claude Code file across all scopes (global and every project), copies them to&#96;~/.claude-backups/latest/&#96;, and commits the diff to a private GitHub repo. The scanner is extracted from Claude Code Organizer, so it knows exactly where to look. Not just&#96;~/.claude/&#96;. It finds project-scoped&#96;.claude/&#96; directories too, and decodes those hashed paths under&#96;~/.claude/projects/&#96;. What it backs up: - Memories (global + per-project) - Skills (full directories, not just manifests) - MCP configurations - Rules - Agents and commands - CLAUDE.md files (global and project-level) - Settings - Plans - Sessions - Plugins The export lands in&#96;~/.claude-backups/latest/&#96; as a clean directory tree. Git tracks it, so you get full version history. If you accidentally delete a rule or a memory gets corrupted, you can diff back to any previous state. ## The automation part This is where it gets actually useful. The tool sets up a systemd timer on Linux or a LaunchAgent on macOS. It runs on boot and again every few hours. The Linux timer has&#96;Persistent=true&#96;, which means if your machine was off when a scheduled run was supposed to happen, it catches up on the next boot. You don't need to remember anything. You don't need it to be running in a terminal somewhere. The setup is one command (check the repo README for exact usage). After that, it just works in the background. ## Why git history matters here Your settings aren't static. Claude Code memories change as you work. You add a rule, then modify it a week later, then realize the old version was better. MCP configs drift. Most backup tools give you a snapshot. Git gives you a timeline. You can see exactly when a setting changed, diff it against any previous version, and restore individual files without rolling back everything. For something like Claude Code memories, which change incrementally over time, this is the right storage model. The private GitHub repo also means your settings follow you across machines. New laptop, new dev container, whatever. Check out the repo and you have your full Claude Code environment back. ## The scanner The part I find interesting technically is the discovery logic. Claude Code doesn't store all project configs in one place. When you work in&#96;/home/you/projects/myapp&#96;, Claude Code creates a directory at&#96;~/.claude/projects/-home-you-projects-myapp/&#96;(the path gets encoded). Then there's also&#96;myapp/.claude/&#96; for project-level settings. The scanner knows both of these patterns. It walks your filesystem looking for&#96;.claude/&#96; directories and cross-references them against the encoded paths in&#96;~/.claude/projects/&#96;. A naive glob would miss half the files. This scanner is the same one that powers Claude Code Organizer. It's been tested on real setups with hundreds of items across many projects,\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The official filename is `settings.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

终端含义：Claude Code 启动后无法连上 Anthropic 官方 API（`api.anthropic.com`），因此报错。官方读取的是 `~/.claude/settings.json`（复数 settings），不是 `setting.json` — 我们之前写错了文件名，配置未被加载。正在把配置复制到 `~/.claude/settings.json`。
